questions.question: What is the smallest prime number?
questions.explanation: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is always equal to 180 degrees.

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a circle?
questions.explanation: The area of a circle can be calculated using the formula πr^2, where r is the radius.

questions.question: What is the largest even number less than 100?
questions.explanation: An even number is any integer that can be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder.

questions.question: What is the sum of all interior angles of a rectangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of all interior angles of a rectangle is always equal to 360 degrees.

questions.question: What is the formula for the volume of a sphere?
questions.explanation: The volume of a sphere can be calculated using the formula (4/3)πr^3, where r is the radius.

questions.question: What is the smallest odd number greater than 10?
questions.explanation: An odd number is any integer that cannot be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder.

questions.question: What is the sum of all interior angles of an octagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of all interior angles of any polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2) × 180, where n is the number of sides.

questions.question: What is the largest odd number less than 50?
questions.explanation: An odd number is any integer that cannot be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder.

questions.question: What is the sum of all interior angles of a hexagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of all interior angles of any polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2) × 180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Prime Numbers
notes.content: A prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.

notes.title: Area of Shapes
notes.content: The area of various shapes can be calculated using different formulas, such as the formula for the area of a circle (πr^2) or the formula for the area of a rectangle (l × w).

notes.title: Volume of Shapes
notes.content: The volume of various shapes can be calculated using different formulas, such as the formula for the volume of a sphere ((4/3)πr^3) or the formula for the volume of a cylinder (πr^2h).

notes.title: Interior Angles of Polygons
notes.content: The sum of all interior angles of any polygon can be calculated using the formula (n-2) × 180, where n is the number of sides.

notes.title: Even and Odd Numbers
notes.content: An even number is any integer that can be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder, while an odd number is any integer that cannot be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder.

notes.title: Types of Triangles
notes.content: There are various types of triangles, including equilateral triangles (all sides equal), isosceles triangles (two sides equal), and scalene triangles (no sides equal).

notes.title: Properties of Circles
notes.content: A circle is a set of points that are all equidistant from a central point called the center. The radius of a circle is the distance from the center to any point on the circumference.

notes.title: Properties of Rectangles
notes.content: A rectangle is a four-sided shape with opposite sides that are equal in length and all angles are right angles. The area of a rectangle can be calculated using the formula l × w.

notes.title: Properties of Spheres
notes.content: A sphere is a three-dimensional shape that is perfectly round, like a globe. The volume of a sphere can be calculated using the formula (4/3)πr^3.

notes.title: Types of Polygons
notes.content: There are various types of polygons, including triangles (three sides), quadrilaterals (four sides), pentagons (five sides), and hexagons (six sides).

notes.title: Properties of Octagons
notes.content: An octagon is an eight-sided polygon. The sum of all interior angles of an octagon can be calculated using the formula (n-2) × 180, where n is the number of sides.

facts.fact: The sum of all interior angles of a triangle is always equal to 180 degrees.
The area of a circle can be calculated using the formula πr^2, where r is the radius.
An even number is any integer that can be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder.
The sum of all interior angles of a rectangle is always equal to 360 degrees.
The volume of a sphere can be calculated using the formula (4/3)πr^3, where r is the radius.
An odd number is any integer that cannot be divided by 2 without leaving a remainder.
The sum of all interior angles of an octagon is always equal to 1080 degrees.
The largest even number less than 100 is 98.
The sum of all interior angles of a hexagon is always equal to 720 degrees.
